programmes.show_description: A Soft Heart Won't Do You No Favours: Chao and Lee band together to face an unexpected threat. Meanwhile, the Hop Wei negotiate with the German traders. (S3, ep 6 of 10)
An emergency dash results in a serious collision between the police van and a taxi. Plus, officers race to save a man threatening to take his own life. (S5, ep 2)
Action-packed documentary following rapid response officers, the fastest cops in Britain, as they crack down on crime. Time is of the essence! (S3, ep 5)
Documentary series following Australia's traffic enforcers. Officers face high-speed chases, defiant hooligans, explosive crashes and out-of-control drunk drivers. (S8, ep 3)
Documentary series following Australia's traffic enforcers. Officers face high-speed chases, defiant hooligans, explosive crashes and out-of-control drunk drivers. (S8, ep 4)
Follow the work of the New Zealand Customs agents in their quest to prevent illegal items passing through the country's borders. (S10, ep 7)
Follow the work of the New Zealand Customs agents in their quest to prevent illegal items passing through the country's borders. (S10, ep 8)
Documentary following the men and women who help keep motorists safe on some of the most perilous highways and byways of New Zealand. (S9, ep 1)
Documentary following the men and women who help keep motorists safe on some of the most perilous highways and byways of New Zealand. (S9, ep 2)
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Police call on multiple units and the Eagle helicopter as they hunt a stolen Subaru and a cyclist can't quite comprehend why he can't bike along the motorway. (S16, ep 9)
A Kiwi stag night ditches the Aussie brother-in-law to be and a car crash leads to some unusual behaviour on the side of the motorway. (S16, ep 10)
Hidden compartments in a traveller's backpack raise red flags, and the discovery of a deadly weapon leaves mail centre workers stunned. (S2, ep 8)
Officers look for a message in a bottle, while a lack of papers land a group of LA rappers in the doghouse. Plus, a young man receives a wake-up call. (S2, ep 9)
Fly-on-the-wall documentary following the men and women who defend Australia from drug runners, smugglers and terrorists. (S5, ep 8)

Officers discover a traveller is driving without a license and a US resident returns from the Dominican Republic with an unusual package. (S1, ep 27)
At Detroit Metro Airport, a Japanese traveller must make a difficult decision. An officer discovers something seedy at the mail facility in Chicago. (S1, ep 28)
Customers uncover concealed drugs. Biosecurity New Zealand find some troublesome items in a spiritual healer's luggage. (S12, ep 3)
A woman is caught body packing highly illegal objects. Meanwhile, there's trouble in Biosecurity. (S12, ep 4)
Fly-on-the-wall documentary following the men and women who defend Australia from drug runners, smugglers and terrorists.  (S4, ep 16)

Armageddon Game: Bashir and O'Brien help to rid two alien races of deadly weapons, unaware that their hosts intend to sacrifice them as part of the peace process.
Beneath the Surface: SG-1 awaken in a mine with no recollection of their former lives. Jonah, Therra, Carlin and Tor - as they are now known - have a new set of memories. (S4, ep 10)
Natural Law: A strange energy barrier causes Chakotay and Seven of Nine's shuttle to crash on a planet inhabited by extremely primitive aliens. (S7, ep 22)
Angel One: The Enterprise crew travels to Angel One, a planet run by women, to search for survivors from a Federation freighter disabled by an asteroid collision seven years earlier.
Officers investigate the suspicious luggage of a man returning from China and Vietnam. Drug detector dog Vulcan makes an amazing find. (S15, ep 15)
Border Force zero in on two men trying to avoid luggage checks as a search of their bags reveals why. There's a million-dollar find in the Mail Centre. (S15, ep 16)
Hand-Off: Officer Nolan finds out his identity has been stolen, potentially threatening his position as an officer. (S2, ep 15)
The Overnight: Officers Chen and Bradford meet Ryan Seacrest and the judges when they respond to a call at the American Idol auditions in LA. (S2, ep 16)
Breaking The Ice: Trip learns that T'Pol has been transmitting secret messages to a nearby Vulcan ship, and Archer attempts to save two crewmen from a disintegrating comet. (S1, ep 8)
Gotta Be Crooked to Get Along in a Crooked World: In the wake of Father Jun's injury, Young Jun steps up for the Hop Wei. (S3, ep 7 of 10)
What appears to be a standard high-speed chase takes a sinister turn when officers search the fugitive's abandoned vehicle. Plus, a fight in a pub gets out of hand. (S5, ep 3)
Action-packed documentary following rapid response officers, the fastest cops in Britain, as they crack down on crime. Time is of the essence! (S3, ep 6)

An angry woman breaks the law but demands police help, a dim car thief is caught on camera and there's the strangest of incidents involving a drive shaft and 11 cars. (S16, ep 11)
Two elderly pedestrians take a wrong turn and dice with death, a taxi driver dumps his passengers on the motorway and police track a car filled with suspected criminals. (S16, ep 12)
An accidental detour leads two Canadians to a dead-end, and an ornate frame holds more than just a picture. Plus, a man is suspected of money laundering. (S2, ep 10)
While an American traveller has trouble recalling his hazy past, bad decisions may keep a rugby player off the pitch. A bag is found in a restricted area, too. (S2, ep 11)

Drunk Smash: Officers face high-speed chases, defiant hooligans, explosive crashes and out-of-control drunk drivers while policing the roads of Australia. (S10, ep 7)
Following Australia's traffic enforcers as they face high-speed chases, explosive crashes and out-of-control drunk drivers. (S10, ep 8)
Cameras follow the men and women of the US border protection agencies as they uphold the country's much debated immigration polices. (S2, ep 1)
Cameras follow the men and women of the US border protection agencies as they uphold the country's much debated immigration polices. (S2, ep 2)
Biosecurity eye rare artefacts from Canada. Two travellers try to outsmart Immigration and there's a sticky situation in Customs. (S12, ep 5)
An irate tourist causes havoc. In Biosecurity, officer Renee uncovers some unwelcome hitchhikers. (S12, ep 6)

Whispers: When O'Brien returns from a security mission he feels that the entire crew has turned against him.
Point Of No Return: Colonel O'Neill meets a strange man who inexplicably possesses intimate knowledge of the Stargate programme. (S4, ep 11)
Homestead: When Voyager discovers a settlement of oppressed Talaxians living on an asteroid, Neelix feels compelled to help them. (S7, ep 23)
11001001: The Enterprise docks for scheduled computer upgrades. When the system is sabotaged, Data, unable to locate Captain Picard or Commander Riker, takes action.
Border Force are concerned when a man travelling from South America doesn't know the combination to open his suitcase. Officers uncover a backpack filled with drugs. (S15, ep 17)
A deadly parcel turns up at the Mail Centre. A suspicious bottle of cologne is investigated, and a couple are caught with a prohibited item that shocks Biosecurity. (S15, ep 18)
Glen McCurley: Examine the crimes of Glen McCurley, who in 1974 abducted 17-year-old Carla Walker from a bowling alley car park and killed her before leaving her body in a ditch. (S9, ep 1)
Emma Baum: The story of 22-year-old Emma Baum, who was savagely battered to death by her ex-boyfriend David Davies in a row over access to their child.
Civilization: On an Earth-like planet, Archer helps a primitive scientist to work out what is killing the townspeople - and he has his first kiss with an alien. (S1, ep 9)
